groupingMethod.problemFormulation: Assess whether simple aromatic hydrocarbon analogues can support exploratory repeated-dose toxicity read-across.

analogues.rationale: Simple aromatic hydrocarbon analogue with related structural and physicochemical features.
Resolved analogue supporting the aromatic hydrocarbon category hypothesis.

includes: OECD QSAR Toolbox grouping/read-across dossier packaging.
Module-local analogue normalization, applicability-domain packaging, and uncertainty-table export.
Portable evidence-matrix packaging for downstream audit and review.
excludes: Cross-module synthesis with non-O-QT evidence sources.
Final hazard, exposure, or risk decisions.

supportedDecisions: Use as typed grouping or read-across evidence input for downstream review.
Use to audit analogue selection, evidence rows, and aspect-level uncertainty packaging.
prohibitedDecisions: Do not treat as final regulatory acceptance of read-across.
Do not treat as a complete evidence-of-record without expert review.

requiredExternalInputs: Expert review of analogue suitability and endpoint relevance.
Downstream evidence synthesis and final decision policy.

inclusionCriteria: Grouping hypothesis: Target and source substances are simple aromatic hydrocarbons expected to share relevant structural and mechanistic features.
Target and source substances must resolve to Toolbox records with stable identifiers and enough comparison data to support analogue evaluation.
Assessment scope is limited to the requested endpoint set: Repeated dose toxicity.
Assessment scope is limited to the stated route of exposure: oral.
allowedDifferences: Differences are only acceptable when the stated grouping hypothesis remains plausible across the assessed similarity contexts and residual uncertainty stays within the accepted level.
boundaryNotes: Assessed 2 target/source pair(s); 0 canonical SMILES exact match(es) and 0 connectivity exact match(es).
Compared 6 shared physicochemical descriptor(s) across 2 target/source pair(s).

rows.summary: Profiler evidence supports a common reactivity/mechanistic framing across the analogue set.
Returned object with keys: value, confidence.

notes: Rows summarize the O-QT evidence matrix assembled for the grouping dossier.

uncertaintyTable.decisionContextFit: Residual uncertainty is acceptable for the stated decision context.

rows.comments: Assessed 2 target/source pair(s); 0 canonical SMILES exact match(es) and 0 connectivity exact match(es).
Compared 6 shared physicochemical descriptor(s) across 2 target/source pair(s).

recommendedActions: Add metabolism simulator evidence when ADME/TK or metabolite similarity is part of the grouping hypothesis.

supportingProfiler.summary: Profiler evidence supports a common reactivity/mechanistic framing across the analogue set.

justification.hypothesis: Target and source substances are simple aromatic hydrocarbons expected to share relevant structural and mechanistic features.
justification.summary: Exploratory read-across dossier assembled with structural, physicochemical, and profiler support; see uncertaintyTable for aspect-level scoring.

endpointConclusions.conclusion: Provisional analogue justification assembled for repeated dose toxicity.

limitations: This object packages O-QT evidence only and is not a final suite-level conclusion.
